2606.23176.title: Synthesizing the Lombard Effect: Multi-Level Control of Speech Clarity and Vocal Effort in TTS
2606.23176.paper_summary: Humans tend to speak louder and clearer in challenging environments, such as noisy conditions or when addressing hearingimpaired listeners, which is called Lombard effect. To simulate this behavior in speech synthesis systems, we introduce a flow-matching based text-to-speech (TTS) model trained with vocal effort and articulation pseudo-labels. The proposed model achieves continuous and disentangled control of vocal effort and articulation, while also enabling word-level emphasis for clarifying specific segments of an utterance. Experimental results show that these control mechanisms effectively improve clarityrelated acoustic features. Furthermore, speech-in-noise experiments demonstrate that our model successfully simulates the intelligibility gains of human clear speech in noisy conditions.
2606.23176.paper_summary_zh: 人類在挑戰性環境中，傾向於講得更大聲且更清晰，例如在嘈雜的條件下或對聽力受損的聽眾講話，這被稱為隆巴德效應。為了在語音合成系統中模擬這種行為，我們提出了一種基於流匹配的文本到語音（TTS）模型，該模型使用聲音努力和發音偽標籤進行訓練。所提出的模型實現了對聲音努力和發音的連續且解耦的控制，同時也能夠對特定語句段落進行單詞級別的強調，以便澄清。實驗結果顯示，這些控制機制有效改善了與清晰度相關的聲學特徵。此外，噪音中的語音實驗表明，我們的模型成功模擬了人類在嘈雜條件下清晰語音的可懂度提升。
2606.23176.author: Seymanur Akti et.al.
